title: Cult Classic Mystery Series That Paved The Way For The X-Files Is Quietly Dominating Streaming
summary: A cult classic mystery series that paved the way for The X-Files is quietly dominating streaming nearly four decades after its premiere.

fullArticle:
Before The X-Files chased the unknown, a cult mystery classic cracked the case, and now it's quietly taking over streaming.

Premiered in 1993, the iconic sci-fi series follows the FBI duo, Fox Mulder (David Duchovny) and Dana Scully (Gillian Anderson), as they investigate unsolved cases involving paranormal phenomena. Before The X-Files became a cultural phenomenon, it drew inspiration from television classics that blended suspense, horror, and speculative sci-fi, such as The Twilight Zone (1959–1964), Night Gallery (1969–1973), Tales from the Darkside (1983–1988), and, above all, Kolchak: The Night Stalker (1974–1975). Now, the cult classic mystery series that paved the way for The X-Files is quietly enjoying a resurgence.
